text: This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: [0] 这里是BBC英语学习的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil. And I'm Beth. It seems people around the world are in love with snacks.
zh: [1] 我是尼尔。我是贝丝。看来世界各地的人们都很爱吃零食。

text: From old favourites like popcorn and crisps to new inventions like the chocolate sandwich,
zh: [2] 从爆米花、薯片这样的老牌零食,到巧克力三明治这样的新发明,

text: snacks are big big business, with an estimated $1 .5 trillion a year spent globally on snacking.
zh: [3] 零食产业规模巨大,据估计全球每年在零食上的花费高达1.5万亿美元。

text: Let's listen as British food historian Annie Gray and Argentinian chef Barbara Ollivary
zh: [4] 让我们来听听英国美食历史学家安妮·格雷和阿根廷厨师芭芭拉·奥利瓦里

text: tell BBC World Service programme The Food Chain what they like to snack on between
zh: [5] 在BBC世界服务节目《食物链》中,谈谈她们喜欢在正餐之间吃什么零食。

text: meals. I like salted peanuts and I love a banana.
zh: [6] 我喜欢咸味花生,也很爱吃香蕉。

text: Preferably together, actually, I find that they hit the spot. My favourite snack is called chipa.
zh: [7] 其实最好是两个一起吃,我觉得这样特别过瘾。我最喜欢的零食叫chipa。

text: It's a small round bun made with cassava, cheese, butter, milk and salt.
zh: [8] 那是一种用木薯粉、奶酪、黄油、牛奶和盐做成的小圆面包。

text: Barbara's favourite snack is a cheese bread called chipa,
zh: [9] 芭芭拉最喜欢的零食是一种叫chipa的奶酪面包,

text: while for Annie it's the combination of peanuts and banana that hits the spot
zh: [10] 而对安妮来说,花生和香蕉的搭配才是最对味的——

text: – an idiom meaning that it's exactly what you want. Neil, what's your favourite snack?
zh: [11] hit the spot这个习语的意思是正是你想要的。尼尔,你最喜欢的零食是什么?

text: Well, I want to say something healthy, but actually, you know, I really love a bag of crisps. Me too.
zh: [12] 我本想说点健康的,但说实话,我真的很爱吃薯片。我也是。

text: Yeah, crisps are good. Well, whatever you like to nibble on, in this episode we'll find out why we are so in love with snack food.
zh: [13] 是啊,薯片很不错。不管你喜欢吃什么零食,这一期我们都要来聊聊,为什么我们如此热爱零食。

text: As usual, we'll learn some useful new words and phrases.
zh: [14] 像往常一样,我们会学到一些实用的新单词和短语。

text: And remember, you'll find all the vocabulary and a quiz on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: [15] 别忘了,你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到所有词汇和小测验。

text: OK, but first I have a question for you, Beth.
zh: [16] 好,不过我先问你一个问题,贝丝。

text: According to a recent YouGov survey, which of the following snacks is most popular amongst Brits?
zh: [17] 根据YouGov最近的一项调查,以下哪种零食在英国人中最受欢迎?

text: Is it… a crisps b confectionery and chocolate or c savoury snacks and biscuits?
zh: [18] 是a薯片,b糖果和巧克力,还是c咸味零食和饼干?

text: Well, we both just said crisps, so I'm going to say crisps.
zh: [19] 嗯,我们俩刚才都说了薯片,那我就选薯片吧。

text: Well, we'll find out the answer later in the programme. Someone with a professional interest in
zh: [20] 好,答案我们稍后揭晓。有一位对零食有专业兴趣的人

text: snacking is Christine Cochrane, president of Snack International, the trade association for the industry.
zh: [21] 是克里斯汀·科克伦,零食国际协会——也就是该行业的贸易协会——的主席。

text: Here, Christine shares her ideas about why we love to snack
zh: [22] 在这里,克里斯汀在BBC世界服务节目《食物链》中

text: with BBC World Service programme, The Food Chain. A snack is an affordable luxury.
zh: [23] 分享了她对我们为何热爱零食的看法。零食是一种负担得起的小奢侈。

text: So if you've had a bad day or money is tight, this is something that you can turn to.
zh: [24] 所以如果你今天过得不顺,或者手头拮据,零食就是你可以求助的东西。

text: And I think that is why you see it as so popular.
zh: [25] 我认为这就是它如此受欢迎的原因。

text: Consumers, especially in the United States, are looking for smaller, more convenient portions.
zh: [26] 消费者,尤其是在美国,都在寻找份量更小、更方便的选择。

text: They're not always going to be able to sit down and have three meals a day, right?
zh: [27] 他们不可能总是坐下来一天吃三顿正餐,对吧?

text: So they're eating on the go. And the other thing that you see is this desire for very specific functional ingredients, right?
zh: [28] 所以他们边走边吃。另外你还会看到人们对某些特定功能性成分的渴望,

text: So mental clarity, gut health, muscle development, these sorts of things.
zh: [29] 比如提升专注力、肠道健康、肌肉发展之类的东西。

text: Again, they're nicely packaged in snack sizes.
zh: [30] 而这些又恰好被巧妙地包装成小份零食。

text: Snacks are often seen as a little luxury, but to be successful, a snack must also be affordable.
zh: [31] 零食常被视为一种小小的奢侈,但要想畅销,零食也必须价格实惠。

text: – an adjective meaning not expensive. Snacks, which are cheap enough for most people to buy,
zh: [32] affordable是个形容词,意思是不贵。这种大多数人都买得起的便宜零食,

text: will sell even if money is tight – a phrase used to describe a situation where there's only just enough money to live on.
zh: [33] 即使手头拮据,也照样卖得出去——money is tight这个短语用来形容钱刚刚够维持生活的状况。

text: Thanks to snacks, today fewer people sit down to eat a proper meal. Instead they eat on the go.
zh: [34] 多亏了零食,如今坐下来好好吃一顿正餐的人越来越少,大家都是边走边吃。

text: If you do something on the go, you do it while you're busy travelling or moving around rather than at home.
zh: [35] 如果你on the go做某事,意思是你在忙着赶路或四处走动时顺便做这件事,而不是在家里做。

text: Many modern snacks target a specific function. For example, an energy drink might claim to improve your gut health
zh: [36] 许多现代零食都主打某种特定功能。比如,一款能量饮料可能宣称能改善你的肠道健康,

text: or your mental clarity – a term describing a state of mind which is focused and engaged, not dull or foggy.
zh: [37] 或者提升你的mental clarity(思维清晰度)——指一种专注、投入,而非迟钝或混沌的精神状态。

text: But not everyone is a fan of modern packaged snacks, such as the bag of crisps and chocolate bars which fill our shops.
zh: [38] 但并非所有人都喜欢现代包装零食,比如摆满商店货架的薯片和巧克力棒。

text: Originally from Argentina, Barbara Ollivary now lives in Florida, USA.
zh: [39] 芭芭拉·奥利瓦里原籍阿根廷,现居美国佛罗里达州。

text: Here, Barbara tells BBC World Service's The Food Chain how she encourages her nine -year -old son to choose more natural snacks.
zh: [40] 在这里,芭芭拉在BBC世界服务《食物链》节目中,讲述了她如何鼓励九岁的儿子选择更天然的零食。

text: Personally, I enjoy cooking and eating more natural, simpler snacks.
zh: [41] 就我个人而言,我更喜欢做一些更天然、更简单的零食来吃。

text: But of course, I can't swim against the current and if my nine -year -old wants to eat the little baked snacks that have cheddar,
zh: [42] 但当然,我不能逆流而上,如果我九岁的孩子想吃那种含切达奶酪的烤零食,

text: ironically, it is something baked with cheese in it, which is similar to what I enjoy. It's just the modern twist to it.
zh: [43] 说来讽刺,那其实是一种烤制的、里面含奶酪的食物,跟我喜欢的东西很像,只是加了点现代的花样。

text: Barbara wants her son to eat healthy, homemade food, although he prefers modern packaged snacks.
zh: [44] 芭芭拉希望儿子吃健康的自制食物,但他更喜欢现代包装零食。

text: Barbara complains she can't swim against the current.
zh: [45] 芭芭拉抱怨说自己没法逆流而上。

text: The idiom swim against the current means to act differently from other people, often because you do not mind being different.
zh: [46] swim against the current这个习语的意思是,做和大多数人不一样的事,通常是因为你并不介意与众不同。

text: Well, Neil, all these snacks have made me hungry. Now, isn't it time to reveal the answer to your question?
zh: [47] 尼尔,这些零食说得我都饿了。现在是不是该公布你那个问题的答案了?

text: Yes it is. I asked, according to a recent YouGov survey, which of the following snacks is the most popular amongst Brits?
zh: [48] 没错。我之前问,根据YouGov最近的一项调查,以下哪种零食在英国人中最受欢迎?

text: Crisps, confectionery and chocolate, or savoury snacks and biscuits?
zh: [49] 薯片,糖果和巧克力,还是咸味零食和饼干?

text: And the answer was... b confectionery and chocolate,
zh: [50] 答案是……b,糖果和巧克力,

text: which 45 % of Britons indulge in weekly, according to that survey. OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learnt,
zh: [51] 根据那项调查,45%的英国人每周都会享用这类零食。好了,我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,

text: starting with the phrase, hits the spot, which describes something which is exactly what you want or need.
zh: [52] 先从hits the spot这个短语说起,它形容某样东西正是你想要或需要的。

text: If something is affordable, it's cheap enough that most people have enough money to buy it.
zh: [53] 如果某样东西是affordable(负担得起的),就是说它便宜到大多数人都买得起。

text: The phrase money is tight means you only have just enough money to live.
zh: [54] money is tight这个短语的意思是,你的钱只够勉强维持生活。

text: The idiom on the go means doing things while you're busy travelling or moving around.
zh: [55] on the go这个习语的意思是在忙着赶路或四处走动时做事。

text: Mental clarity is a state in which your mind is fully focused and active, not dull or foggy.
zh: [56] mental clarity(思维清晰)是指你的头脑完全专注、活跃,而不是迟钝或混沌的一种状态。

text: And finally, people who swim against the current or the tide do or say things differently from most other people.
zh: [57] 最后,swim against the current或the tide,指的是人做事或说话方式与大多数人不同。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but why not head over to our website, bbclearningenglish .com,
zh: [58] 我们的六分钟又到时间了,不过别忘了访问我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com,

text: to try the quiz and worksheet for this episode. See you again soon. Goodbye.
zh: [59] 去试试这期节目的小测验和练习题吧。我们下次再见,再见。
